Class AIPlayer

java.lang.Object
com.cluegame.players.Player
com.cluegame.players.AIPlayer
public class AIPlayer extends Player
Represents an autonomous AI player in Clue! Moves toward rooms, makes random suggestions and uses a DetectiveNotepad to track seen cards and deduce when to make an accusation.
Author:
Thanh Shaw

  • Constructor Details

    • AIPlayer

      public AIPlayer(String name, String token, int startRow, int startCol)
      Constructs an AI player with a name, token and starting position.
      Parameters:
      name - the player's name
      token - the player's token identifier
      startRow - starting row on the board
      startCol - starting column on the board

  • Method Details

    • addCard

      public void addCard(Card card)
      Overrides addCard to also record the card in the detective notepad. Cards in the AI's own hand cannot be in the murder envelope.
      Overrides:
      addCard in class Player
      Parameters:
      card - the card to add

    • takeTurn

      public void takeTurn(Board board, Dice dice, List<Player> allPlayers)
      Takes a turn by rolling the dice and moving toward the nearest room. If already in a room, the AI will sometimes use a secret passage or otherwise exit through a random available door and keep moving.
      Specified by:
      takeTurn in class Player
      Parameters:
      board - the game board
      dice - the dice to roll
      allPlayers - all players for corridor occupancy checks

    • makeSuggestion

      public Suggestion makeSuggestion()
      Makes a suggestion using the current room. Prioritises unseen suspects and weapons to gather new information, avoids repeating the exact same suggestion from the previous turn, and targets the deduced envelope card when a category is fully narrowed.
      Specified by:
      makeSuggestion in class Player
      Returns:
      a Suggestion using the current room, or null if not in a room

    • pickBest

      private String pickBest(String[] names, String lastSuggested)
      Picks the best name to suggest from a category. Priority order: 1. The deduced envelope card (if exactly one unseen remains) 2. An unseen name that was not suggested last turn 3. Any unseen name 4. The deduced missing card (if known) 5. A random name as a last resort
      Parameters:
      names - all names in the category
      lastSuggested - the name suggested last turn (to avoid repeats)
      Returns:
      the chosen name

    • setUndisprovedSuggestion

      public void setUndisprovedSuggestion(Suggestion suggestion)
      Records a suggestion that no one could disprove. The AI treats this as a strong accusation candidate on future turns.
      Parameters:
      suggestion - the undisproved suggestion

    • makeAccusation

      public Accusation makeAccusation()
      Makes an accusation only when the AI has enough confirmed information. Confirmed cards can come from normal notepad deduction or from undisproved suggestions where the AI can rule out its own hand.
      Specified by:
      makeAccusation in class Player
      Returns:
      an Accusation if the AI is ready, or null

    • chooseSuggestionCard

      public Card chooseSuggestionCard(List<Card> matchingCards, String askerName)
      Automatically shows the first matching card to the suggesting player.
      Specified by:
      chooseSuggestionCard in class Player
      Parameters:
      matchingCards - cards in hand that match the suggestion
      askerName - the player who made the suggestion
      Returns:
      the first matching card

    • seeDisprovalCard

      public void seeDisprovalCard(Card card, String fromPlayerName)
      Records a shown card in the detective notepad for future deduction.
      Specified by:
      seeDisprovalCard in class Player
      Parameters:
      card - the card being shown
      fromPlayerName - the player showing the card

    • getNotepad

      public DetectiveNotepad getNotepad()
      Returns the AI's detective notepad.
      Returns:
      the notepad tracking seen cards

    • markVisitedRoom

      public void markVisitedRoom(String roomName)
      Records that the AI has physically visited a room. This is used only for movement/exploration preferences and must not be confused with seeing a room card.
      Parameters:
      roomName - the room that was visited

    • getVisitedRooms

      public Set<String> getVisitedRooms()
      Returns the set of rooms the AI has visited during play.
      Returns:
      visited room names

    • getConfirmedRoomName

      public String getConfirmedRoomName()
      Returns the confirmed murder room, if the AI has deduced it.
      Returns:
      confirmed room name, or null if still unknown

    • couldRoomBeEnvelope

      public boolean couldRoomBeEnvelope(String roomName)
      Returns true if this room card is still useful evidence for the AI. Rooms already in the AI's hand or shown by another player cannot be the murder room, so moving there is lower value.
      Parameters:
      roomName - the room to evaluate
      Returns:
      true if the room could still be in the envelope

    • holdsCardNamed

      private boolean holdsCardNamed(String cardName)

    • suggestionContainsName

      private boolean suggestionContainsName(Suggestion suggestion, String cardName)

    • learnFromUndisprovedSuggestion

      private boolean learnFromUndisprovedSuggestion(Suggestion suggestion)

    • isEnvelopeCandidate

      private boolean isEnvelopeCandidate(String cardName)

    • findBestDirection

      private int[] findBestDirection(Board board, List<Player> allPlayers)
      Picks the best direction to move by trying to get closer to the nearest room door. If no direction gets closer, picks a random valid one instead.
      Parameters:
      board - the game board
      allPlayers - all players for occupancy checks
      Returns:
      int array {dRow, dCol} for the chosen step, or null if stuck

    • findNearestDoor

      private Square findNearestDoor(Board board)
      Finds the nearest room door on the board, preferring rooms the AI has not yet visited (to gather information about more rooms). Falls back to the overall nearest door if all rooms have been visited.
      Parameters:
      board - the game board
      Returns:
      the nearest door Square, or null if none found

    • findFreeDoor

      private Square findFreeDoor(Room room, List<Player> allPlayers)
      Finds a random unblocked door in the given room.
      Parameters:
      room - the room to exit from
      allPlayers - all players for occupancy checks
      Returns:
      a free door Square, or null if all doors are blocked
